menu.ai_settings.help_message:
To use the AI translator, configure the appropriate parameters in the Default file (use the 'Edit Active Prompt' option) or create a new .txt file in the prompts directory (use the 'Open Prompts Folder' option). The file name will automatically be treated as the prompt name.

You can also create special prompts – simply add a # character at the beginning of the file name. They will be visible in the results window menu and can be used, for example, for additional text analysis or checking the definition of a selected fragment.
result_popup.footer: LMB – close  ·  RMB – copy translation  ·  Alt+RMB – copy original
result_popup.tooltip.special_prompts:
Select a special prompt (by creating a file with '#' prefix in AI translator settings)
to get additional analysis or process text according to your own rules defined in the prompt.
result_popup.no_special_prompts: (no special prompts; define files with '#' prefix in AI translator settings)
network.error.llm_unavailable: (AI connection error — check your configuration and server availability, details in alliulingo.log)
help_window.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
prompt.comment.endpoint:
API server destination address (Endpoint).
For local models (e.g. LM Studio): usually http://127.0.0.1:1234/v1/chat/completions
For external APIs (OpenAI standard): official provider URL
Example for Groq Cloud: https://api.groq.com/openai/v1/chat/completions
prompt.comment.api_key:
Unique authentication key (API Key / Token).
For local models: field can remain empty.
For external APIs: required field. Used to authorize your account with the service provider.
prompt.comment.model:
AI Model Identifier (Model ID).
Specifies which specific "brain" AI you want to use.
For local models: name displayed in LM Studio (e.g. google/gemma-4-e4b).
For external APIs: technical provider name (e.g. llama-3.3-70b-versatile).
prompt.comment.temperature:
Temperature parameter (Model creativity).
Range usually from 0.0 to 1.0 (some models up to 2.0).
Lower values (e.g. 0.1 - 0.2) make the translation more literal and repeatable.
Higher values increase linguistic freedom, which in technical translations may favor errors.
prompt.comment.translator_section: This section determines the quality of the translation.
prompt.comment.skill:
System instruction (System Prompt / Skill).
Here you define the AI role. A well-formulated 'skill' should include:
1) Role: (e.g. professional strategy games translator).
2) Context: Information about the game title or field (e.g. Distant Worlds 2 terminology).
3) Glossary: Explicit instructions for specific words (which not to translate).
4) Formatting: Order to return clean text without comments and explanations.

DYNAMIC PARAMETERS:
[sLang] - will be automatically replaced by the source language code (e.g. eng, pol, deu).
[tLang] - will be automatically replaced by the target language code (e.g. eng, pol, deu).
prompt.comment.user_content_prefix:
Optional user message prefix (User Content Prefix).
Added directly before the text to be translated in each query.
Helps the AI model uniquely distinguish the work text from system instructions.
Example: "TEXT TO TRANSLATE:" or "TRANSLATE:"
prompt.default.skill_value: You are a professional translator. Translate from [sLang] to [tLang]. Return only the clean translation, without any additional comments, explanations or quotes.
error.ocr.message: (language file not found — %1.traineddata in tessdata directory)
